title: IBM RS/6000 SP RAMP: Market Research Proposal
author: Peter S. Kastner
abstract: This proposal outlines a dual-objective Aberdeen Group market research engagement for IBM's RS/6000 SP parallel system: producing a commercial customer case-study white paper for the IBM sales force, while simultaneously investigating evidence that the SP has been mis-sold at some accounts with unrealistically high customer expectations. Aberdeen proposes structured telephone and face-to-face interviews with SP commercial customers to surface best-practice application profiles and, where present, diagnose the extent of any customer dissatisfaction problem before it becomes a broader issue for IBM.
importance_rationale: Documents the research methodology Aberdeen used for combined marketing-research and customer-satisfaction engagements with major IT vendors, and captures early commercial adoption dynamics of massively parallel processing systems.
relevance_rationale: The RS/6000 SP platform and the specific market conditions of 1995 are historical; the document has limited ongoing applicability beyond illustrating consulting engagement design.
prescience_rationale: This is a consulting research proposal rather than a market analysis or prediction; prescience assessment is not applicable.
